title: قرار رقم 21 لسنة 2009: بشأن إعتماد وإصدار السياسة التنظيمية بشأن تكاليف تطبيق خدمة نقل الأرقام

provisions.content: (8) of Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003 regarding the Organizationof the Telecommunications Sector grants the TRA the power, inter alia, to issueregulations, orders, resolutions and procedures in relation to Mobile NumberPortability.1.2. TheTRA has issued the Regulatory Policy for Number Portability and the MobileNumber Portability Instructions.1.3Paragraph 4.6 of the Mobile Number Portability Instructions provides that:"the TRAshall from time to time, in its absolute discretion either:4.6.1.recover from the Licensees (and if applicable, from any New Licensees);or4.6.2require payment in advance from the Licensees (and if applicable, from any NewLicensees),theamount of any costs, charges, fees and expenses incurred directly or indirectlyby the TRA as a result of the introduction of MNP into the UAE, including anycosts, charges, fees, and expenses incurred by the TRA under the MNP Contract.The TRA may do this through any mechanism it sees fit, including but not limitedto the imposition of MNP Regulatory Fees.TheLicensees (and if applicable, any New Licensees) shall pay any MNP RegulatoryFees strictly as provided for by the TRA."1.4 Allwords, terms and phrases used in this Regulatory Policy shall have the meaningsascribed to them, if any, in the Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003 regardingthe Organization of the Telecommunications Sector or the TRA's Mobile NumberPortability Instructions, from time to time as the case may be, or anyreplacement for either of them, unless otherwise provided for in this RegulatoryPolicy.1.5 ThisRegulatory Policy should be read in conjunction with the Regulatory Policy forNumber Portability and the Mobile Number Portability Instructions, PROVIDED THATin the event of any inconsistency between the provisions of either or both ofthose instruments and this Regulatory Policy, this Regulatory Policy shallprevail as regards those matters the subject of it.2. Scope2.1 ThisRegulatory Policy sets out the details of the MNP Regulatory Fees.2.2Through the imposition of the MNP Regulatory Fees, the TRA is recovering theamount of costs, charges, fees and expenses incurred directly or indirectly bythe TRA as a result of the introduction of MNP into the UAE, including anycosts, charges, fees, and expenses incurred by the TRA under the MNPContract.2.3 TheMNP Regulatory Fees are payable by the Licensees at the time and in the mannerdescribed in paragraph "3. The Policy" of this Regulatory Policy.3. The Policy3.1Subject to paragraph 3.6, the MNP Regulatory Fees for the first five yearsfollowing the date of this Regulatory Policy shall be payable in installments,each of which installments is set out in Annexure 1 to this Regulatory Policy(the "Installments").3.2 Eachof the Licensees shall pay each of the Installments within 15 calendar days ofreceipt of an invoice for the same issued by the TRA.3.3 TheTRA shall use its best endeavors to issue an invoice to each of the Licenseesfor the first Installment (being Installment 1 as set out in Annexure 1 to thisRegulatory Policy) within 7 calendar days of this Regulatory Policy.3.4 TheTRA shall use its best endeavors to issue an invoice in respect of each otherInstallment (being Installments 2 to 5 as set out in Annexure 1 to thisRegulatory Policy), no later than 15 calendar days prior to the date ofcommencement of the year in respect of which the relevant Installment is to bepaid (as set out In Annexure 1 to this Regulatory Policy).3.5 Whilethe TRA will use its best endeavours to issue the invoices at the times referredto in paragraphs 3.3 and 3.4 above, if the TRA issues those invoices at anyother time, the Licensees will still be obliged to pay those invoices within 15calendar days of receipt of them.3.6Notwithstanding anything else in this Regulatory Policy the TRA reserves theright to, at any time, issue invoices for an MNP Regulatory Fee (other than theInstallments), to recover the amount of any costs, charges, fees and expensesincurred directly or indirectly by the TRA as a result of the introduction andimplementation of MNP into the UAE, which have not be accounted for in thecalculation of the Installments.4. Effective DateThisRegulatory Policy shall come into effect on the day that it is issued by theTRA.5. PublicationThe TRAreserves the right to, in its absolute discretion, publish this RegulatoryPolicy. Until the TRA exercises its discretion to do so, the Licensees shallregard it as confidential as between the TRA and each of the Licensees.6. RevocationThe TRAreserves the right to replace, modify or reissue this Regulatory Policy at anytime as it sees fit.Annexure 1Installmentspayable by each of the Licensees, in respect of each of the five years followingthe date of this Regulatory PolicyInstallmentAmount of InstallmentYear in respect of whichInstallment is to be paidInstallment 16,894,940.80Year 1 (being the 12 monthperiod commencing on the date of this Regulatory Policy)Installment 21,861,622.40Year 2 (being the 12 monthperiod commencing on the day immediately following the expiry of Year 1)Installment 31,861,622.40Year 3 ((being the 12 monthperiod commencing on the day immediately following the expiry of Year 2)Installment 41,861,622.40Year 4 (being the 12 monthperiod commencing on the day immediately following the expiry of Year 3)Installment 51,861,622.40Year 5 (being the 12 monthperiod commencing on the day immediately following the expiry of Year 4)Total amount payable inInstallments 1 to 514,341,430.40[1]لميرد التاريخالهجري لهذاالقرارالمنشور فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم 497ص411،فاقتضىالتنويه.
